compiled_state.title: fix-bug and debug-everything miscategorize LLM-behavioral bugs as mechanical — skip investigation and bot-psychologist
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What happened

During a debug-everything session (2026-03-30), the orchestrator treated 15+ skill/workflow/prompt bugs as mechanical text fixes rather than LLM-behavioral bugs requiring bot-psychologist investigation. The agent rationalized this by framing skill file changes as 'find wrong text, replace it' without running the dual-signal detection or dispatching bot-psychologist.

## Observed rationalization

The orchestrator explicitly noted: 'I've been treating them as mechanical text fixes — find the wrong text, replace it. That works for truly mechanical issues (wrong step number, wrong flag syntax), but for the behavioral bugs (agent spiraling, review escalation not triggering, wrong dispatch decisions), bot-psychologist's 15-point failure taxonomy and iterative hypothesis-experiment loop would produce a more rigorous diagnosis.'

This rationalization reveals the core issue: the mechanical classification is too broad. Agents categorize any 'obvious-looking' fix as mechanical to avoid the investigation overhead, even when the bug is about LLM behavioral gaps.

## Also observed in host projects

This miscategorization pattern is not limited to the DSO plugin — it has been observed in host projects as well, confirming it is a systemic issue in the classification logic.

## Root cause

The mechanical error definition in fix-bug SKILL.md (lines 60-66) lists 'config syntax — malformed YAML, TOML, JSON, or conf file' as mechanical, but skill files (.md) are not conf files. The broader issue: any fix where the agent can see 'what text is wrong and what it should be' gets classified as mechanical, bypassing investigation entirely. The definition needs to be narrowed to truly deterministic fixes where:
1. The error message points to the exact fix (e.g., 'ImportError: cannot import name X')
2. There is exactly ONE valid fix (no judgment involved)
3. The fix does NOT change agent behavior or guidance

## Fix needed

1. Narrow the mechanical error definition in fix-bug SKILL.md to exclude skill/agent/prompt files entirely from mechanical classification
2. Add an explicit guard: if the bug affects a file in skills/, agents/, or prompts/, it MUST be classified as llm-behavioral (dual-signal met by definition — the file type signal is present, and the bug is about the behavioral outcome of that file)
3. Consider adding a 'mechanical trap' warning: 'If the fix looks simple but involves changing how an agent behaves, it is NOT mechanical — classify as llm-behavioral'

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral. Dual-signal: ticket describes LLM misclassification behavior + affected file is skills/fix-bug/SKILL.md.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: SAFEGUARD APPROVED: user approved editing plugins/dso/skills/fix-bug/SKILL.md. Proposed fix: add mandatory file-directory pre-screen before mechanical classification.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
